title: object_storage_bucket
description: One object storage bucket or container in any cloud — AWS S3, GCS, Azure Blob Storage, MinIO, or any S3-compatible store. The single cross-vendor shape policies use for encryption-at-rest, public-access, and lifecycle checks. Plugins normalize provider-specific encryption modes and access-control configurations into the boolean fields here; rules consume the same payload regardless of source.

name.description: Bucket / container name as the provider sees it.

region_or_location.description: Provider's region or multi-region location string (us-east-1, EU, …). Free-form — semantics differ per cloud.

encryption_at_rest_enabled.description: Server-side encryption at rest is on. Plugins should set true when ANY encryption mode is configured (SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, GCS default, Azure Storage Service Encryption, customer-managed key, etc.).

kms_managed.description: Encryption uses a customer-managed key (CMK) rather than provider-default. Optional; absent means unknown or provider-default.

kms_key_id.description: Opaque provider-specific identifier of the customer-managed key when kms_managed is true.

public_access_blocked.description: Bucket-level configuration prevents the bucket and its objects from being made public. Maps to: S3 PublicAccessBlock all-true, GCS UBLA + PublicAccessPrevention=enforced, Azure allow_blob_public_access=false.

versioning_enabled.description: Object versioning / soft-delete is on. Optional; absent means unknown.
